title: Seizure Disorders & Epilepsy
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Seizure Disorders & Epilepsy
Identify key diagnostic criteria for Seizure Disorders & Epilepsy
Recognize complications and when to escalate care

sections.title: Overview & Pathophysiology
sections.content: **Seizure Disorders & Epilepsy** — This section covers the fundamental pathophysiology, epidemiology, and clinical significance. Understanding the underlying mechanisms is essential for clinical reasoning and evidence-based management.

sections.title: Clinical Presentation & Diagnosis
sections.content: Key clinical features, diagnostic criteria, and workup for Seizure Disorders & Epilepsy. This section emphasizes pattern recognition and systematic diagnostic approaches.

sections.title: Management & Treatment
sections.content: Evidence-based management strategies for Seizure Disorders & Epilepsy, including first-line therapies, escalation pathways, and monitoring parameters.

sections.title: Complications & Follow-Up
sections.content: Potential complications, prognosis, and longitudinal management considerations for Seizure Disorders & Epilepsy.

keyTakeaways: Recognize the classic presentation of Seizure Disorders & Epilepsy
Identify red flags requiring urgent intervention

references.title: Radiopaedia: CT Head (acute) → MRI Brain Cases

reviewQuestions.question: Which of the following is the MOST important initial step in evaluating a patient with suspected Seizure Disorders & Epilepsy?
options: Obtain a thorough history and physical examination
Consult a specialist before any evaluation
reviewQuestions.explanation: A thorough history and physical examination is always the cornerstone of clinical evaluation for Seizure Disorders & Epilepsy, guiding subsequent diagnostic workup and management decisions.

reviewQuestions.question: What is the MOST critical factor in determining the management approach for Seizure Disorders & Epilepsy?
reviewQuestions.explanation: Clinical severity and appropriate risk stratification guide management decisions in Seizure Disorders & Epilepsy, determining the level of intervention and monitoring required.

reviewQuestions.question: Which complication of Seizure Disorders & Epilepsy requires the MOST urgent intervention?
reviewQuestions.explanation: Hemodynamic instability in the setting of Seizure Disorders & Epilepsy requires immediate intervention including fluid resuscitation, close monitoring, and potentially escalation to critical care.

imaging_reasoning.order_reasoning: CT in ED for first seizure/new-onset; MRI epilepsy protocol for recurrent/unexplained
imaging_reasoning.expected_findings: CT: may show mass, hemorrhage, calcification; MRI: hippocampal sclerosis, cortical dysplasia, tumor
imaging_reasoning.management_impact: Structural lesion = consider surgery; normal MRI = medical management; status epilepticus imaging after stabilization
